predictions.text: By 2026-06-30, a U.S. federal court will issue a preliminary injunction (or temporary restraining order) that blocks enforcement of the President’s executive order limiting mail-in voting in at least one material provision.
predictions.verification_criteria: A publicly accessible federal court order (district court or appellate) explicitly grants a TRO or preliminary injunction against the executive order (or implementing federal action) and states that enforcement is enjoined/suspended for at least one substantive requirement related to mail-in voting.
monitoring_signals: PACER filings naming the EO in complaints by states/NGOs
DOJ Civil Division filings defending the EO
Emergency motions for TRO/PI in D.D.C. or circuit courts
Press releases from state AG coalitions
predictions.rationale: High-salience election administration actions commonly draw rapid litigation and early injunctive relief when plaintiffs show likely irreparable harm before elections.

predictions.text: By 2026-04-30, at least 15 U.S. states will have joined (as plaintiffs or intervenors) at least one lawsuit challenging the federal executive order limiting mail-in voting.
predictions.verification_criteria: Court docket(s) show 15+ distinct U.S. states listed as plaintiffs and/or granted intervenor status in a case whose complaint explicitly challenges the mail-in voting executive order (or its implementing federal directives).
monitoring_signals: Multi-state AG press conferences/complaints
Amended complaints adding additional states
Motions to intervene filed by states
Major election-law organizations announcing coordinated suits
predictions.rationale: Multi-state coalitions are a common tactic for high-profile federal actions affecting state-run election administration.

predictions.text: By 2026-05-31, CISA will publish a public alert/advisory (AA, CSA, or equivalent) that explicitly references the malicious NPM 'axios' package compromise and provides defensive guidance (IOCs, mitigations, or detection steps).
predictions.verification_criteria: A CISA web-posted advisory or alert explicitly includes the words 'axios' and 'npm' (or 'Node Package Manager') and describes the compromise as malicious/trojanized, with actionable mitigations or indicators.
monitoring_signals: CISA Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) entries related to Node supply chain incidents
Joint CSA publications (CISA/FBI/NSA) on software supply chain
GitHub Security Advisories referencing axios incident
Major EDR vendors publishing IOCs tied to axios RAT behavior
predictions.rationale: Large-scale, widely used dependency compromises frequently trigger government advisories due to broad downstream exposure.

predictions.text: By 2026-12-31, NPM (npmjs.com) or GitHub will announce and implement a new default supply-chain security control for JavaScript packages: either mandatory provenance attestation/signing for new releases OR mandatory 2FA for all maintainers of packages above 1 million weekly downloads.
predictions.verification_criteria: A public announcement and corresponding policy/docs update from NPM or GitHub states a new requirement (provenance/attestation or 2FA) and indicates it is enforced (blocked publishes or account requirements) for the specified scope (new releases or >1M weekly downloads).
monitoring_signals: NPM/GitHub blog posts on provenance, Sigstore, or account security
Changes to npm CLI publish error messages regarding provenance/2FA
Policy updates in GitHub Actions / trusted publishing docs
Post-incident reports from NPM on ecosystem security
predictions.rationale: High-profile package compromises often accelerate platform-wide hardening measures, especially for the most-downloaded packages.

predictions.text: By 2026-12-31, Oracle will report a restructuring-related charge of at least $1.0 billion in a single quarter (GAAP), explicitly tied to layoffs/reorganization, in a Form 10-Q/10-K or earnings release.
predictions.verification_criteria: Oracle quarterly/annual filings or earnings materials disclose restructuring/reorganization charges (or severance/termination costs) of >=$1.0B for a quarter, with narrative linking to workforce reduction or organizational restructuring.
monitoring_signals: Oracle 10-Q footnotes on restructuring accruals
Earnings-call comments on workforce actions and cost realignment
Large WARN notices or aggregated layoff trackers confirming scale
Analyst notes flagging one-time charges
predictions.rationale: If layoffs are broad and tied to major strategic reallocation toward AI capex, disclosed charges can become large and concentrated in a quarter.

predictions.text: By 2026-07-31, Microsoft will announce an increase of at least $10 billion in its full-year capex guidance (or expected capex range midpoint) versus its most recently stated full-year capex guidance as of 2026-03-31, explicitly citing AI/data-center capacity needs.
predictions.verification_criteria: In an earnings release, investor presentation, or call transcript, Microsoft provides an updated full-year capex expectation that is >=$10B higher than the most recent full-year guidance/expectation stated on or before 2026-03-31, and management attributes the change to AI/data-center buildout.
monitoring_signals: MSFT earnings call capex commentary and updated ranges
Azure capacity constraint commentary and lead times
Supplier capex and server/accelerator procurement signals
Data-center lease and power procurement announcements
predictions.rationale: Investor focus on AI infrastructure and margins raises the odds Microsoft will clarify or expand capex plans within the next two quarters.

predictions.text: By 2026-06-30, the 2026 U.S. Social Security Trustees Report will project the OASI trust fund depletion year as 2033 or earlier (i.e., ≤2033).
predictions.verification_criteria: The 2026 Trustees Report (PDF or tables) lists the OASI trust fund 'depletion' (or 'exhaustion') year, and the stated year is 2033 or earlier.
monitoring_signals: SSA/Trustees publication date announcements
CBO long-term budget outlook updates
Revisions to wage growth, immigration, or interest-rate assumptions
Media pre-briefs on trustees projections
predictions.rationale: Recent reporting emphasizes a widening financing gap; modest adverse assumption shifts can keep depletion timing at or earlier than recent baselines.

predictions.text: By 2026-08-31, the UN Security Council will adopt a resolution renewing UNIFIL that includes at least one new or strengthened operative paragraph on countering IED/roadside-bomb threats or improving UNIFIL force protection/freedom of movement compared with the prior renewal resolution.
predictions.verification_criteria: A UN Security Council resolution renewing UNIFIL (adopted by 2026-08-31) contains operative language explicitly referencing IEDs/roadside bombs (or 'explosive devices') and/or specifies enhanced force-protection measures or reporting requirements that are not present (or are materially expanded) versus the immediately preceding UNIFIL renewal resolution.
monitoring_signals: UNSC draft resolution negotiations and press statements
UN Secretary-General reports on UNIFIL incidents
Lebanon/Israel diplomatic notes about UNIFIL mandate
Subsequent attacks or increased incident tempo
predictions.rationale: Fatal attacks on peacekeepers often catalyze mandate language changes emphasizing protection and mobility, especially at scheduled renewal points.

predictions.text: By 2026-12-31, ASTM or ISO will publish a publicly accessible guidance document (standard, technical report, or committee draft) on microplastics sampling/analysis that explicitly warns that common lab gloves can contaminate samples and recommends mitigation (e.g., glove material choice, rinsing, or procedural blanks).
predictions.verification_criteria: An ASTM or ISO document (published or publicly posted draft) on microplastics includes explicit language linking gloves to microplastic contamination risk and provides at least one recommended procedural mitigation; the document must be identifiable by standard number or ISO/ASTM committee posting.
monitoring_signals: ASTM/ISO committee agendas and draft postings on microplastics methods
Method-validation studies replicating glove contamination findings
Funding calls or interlaboratory comparisons (ILCs) referencing glove artifacts
Journal methods editorials recommending procedural blanks/material controls
predictions.rationale: If glove-derived contamination is a meaningful systematic error, standards bodies typically incorporate mitigation guidance as methods mature and replication accumulates.
